senses.definition: A traditional Arabic name for a fixed star in the constellation Cygnus, used mainly in historical astronomy and astrology.
examples: In some old star charts, Deneb is labeled as Azelfafage.
The astrologer explained that Azelfafage was rising on the eastern horizon.
Medieval scholars sometimes mentioned Azelfafage when describing the night sky.

senses.definition: A liquid mixture whose vapour has the same composition as the liquid when it boils, so its parts cannot be separated by simple distillation.
examples: Ethanol and water can form an azeotrope that limits how pure the alcohol can become by distillation.
The engineer modified the process to break the azeotrope and recover both components.
Azeotrope formation is an important challenge in industrial chemical separation.

senses.definition: Plural of azeotrope: several liquid mixtures whose vapour has the same composition as the liquid when they boil, so their parts cannot be separated by simple distillation.
examples: Many industrial solvents form azeotropes with water.
Researchers classified the known azeotropes for this system.
Different azeotropes may appear as pressure and temperature change.

senses.definition: Relating to or having the properties of an azeotrope.
examples: They used azeotropic distillation to remove the water.
The azeotropic mixture boils at a constant temperature.
Azeotropic behavior complicates the design of the separation process.

senses.definition: Plural of azeotropy: several cases or types of the property of forming an azeotrope.
examples: Different azeotropies appear in multi-component mixtures.
The paper reviews known azeotropies in alcohol–water systems.
Engineers must understand possible azeotropies before designing the column.

senses.definition: The tendency of certain liquid mixtures to form an azeotrope.
examples: Azeotropism makes it hard to separate the components completely.
The researcher explored azeotropism in ternary mixtures.
A good model of azeotropism helps design better distillation processes.

senses.definition: The property of a liquid mixture that forms an azeotrope and boils without changing its composition.
examples: Azeotropy limits the purity that can be achieved by distillation.
The azeotropy of the mixture occurs at a specific composition.
By changing the pressure, they tried to remove the azeotropy.

senses.definition: Relating to Azerbaijan, its people, or its language; less common than 'Azerbaijani'.
examples: He enjoyed listening to Azerbaijanese music during his trip.
The museum had an exhibition of Azerbaijanese art.
Some older books still use the term Azerbaijanese instead of Azerbaijani.

senses.definition: A person from Azerbaijan or of Azerbaijani origin; less common than 'Azerbaijani'.
examples: Many Azerbaijanese now live and work abroad.
She met an Azerbaijanese who spoke several languages fluently.
The writer is an Azerbaijanese with a strong interest in history.

senses.definition: Relating to Azerbaijan, its people, or its language.
examples: She is studying Azerbaijani literature at university.
Azerbaijani culture has been influenced by both East and West.
The restaurant serves traditional Azerbaijani dishes.

senses.definition: A person from Azerbaijan or of Azerbaijani origin, or the Turkic language spoken in Azerbaijan and parts of Iran.
examples: An Azerbaijani greeted us warmly at the market.
Millions of Azerbaijanis live in both Azerbaijan and Iran.
He speaks Azerbaijani at home and Russian at work.

senses.definition: Relating to Azerbaijan, its people, or its language; a less common variant of 'Azerbaijani'.
examples: The report described Azerbaijanian economic reforms.
Older texts sometimes use the term Azerbaijanian instead of Azerbaijani.
She researched Azerbaijanian history for her thesis.

senses.definition: A person from Azerbaijan; a less common variant of 'Azerbaijani'.
examples: We spoke with an Azerbaijanian about daily life in Baku.
The author is an Azerbaijanian living in Europe.
Several Azerbaijanians attended the conference.

senses.definition: A type of computer or typewriter keyboard layout used mainly in French-speaking countries, where the first six letters on the top row are A-Z-E-R-T-Y.
examples: French users are often more comfortable with an AZERTY keyboard.
He switched his laptop from AZERTY to QWERTY.
Typing special characters can be different on an AZERTY layout.

senses.definition: A traditional name, from Arabic, used in some lists of stars for a star in the constellation Eridanus; mainly of historical or specialist interest.
examples: Some old star catalogues mention a star called Azha in Eridanus.
The name Azha is rarely used in modern astronomy.
He studied the history of star names such as Azha.

senses.definition: A chemical compound that contains the group N3, often highly reactive and sometimes explosive.
examples: Sodium azide is used in car airbags but must be handled carefully.
The lab stores azide compounds in a special cabinet.
Accidental contact with heavy-metal azide can be very dangerous.

senses.definition: Plural of azide: several chemical compounds that contain the group N3, often highly reactive and sometimes explosive.
examples: Heavy metal azides are extremely sensitive to shock.
The company produces various organic azides for research.
Azides play an important role in click chemistry.

senses.definition: Describing a molecule that contains an azide group, especially as part of its name in chemistry.
examples: They prepared an azido derivative of the sugar.
Azido groups are often used as reactive handles in biochemistry.
The azido compound was stored at low temperature.

senses.definition: A drug used to treat HIV infection, also known as zidovudine or AZT.
examples: Azidothymidine was one of the first drugs used against HIV.
The doctor adjusted the dose of azidothymidine because of side effects.
Combination therapy often includes azidothymidine with other antivirals.

senses.definition: Plural of azidothymidine: several doses, forms, or types of this HIV treatment drug.
examples: Different azidothymidines were tested in the clinical trial.
The study compared generic azidothymidines with the original brand.
Researchers observed how various azidothymidines affected viral load.

senses.definition: A chemical compound related to ethane that contains a nitrogen atom in a three‑membered ring, used mainly in specialist organic chemistry.
examples: The synthesis of aziethane requires careful control of conditions.
Aziethane derivatives are of interest for their unusual ring strain.
Only small amounts of aziethane were isolated from the reaction.

senses.definition: Relating to a late Stone Age culture in western Europe, known for painted pebbles and other small objects.
examples: Azilian art often includes simple designs on small stones.
The archaeologist found tools typical of the Azilian period.
They compared Azilian sites across France and Spain.

senses.definition: A person who belonged to the Azilian culture of the late Stone Age.
examples: The museum display showed how Azilians decorated pebbles.
Azilians lived mainly by hunting and gathering.
Some scholars debate how Azilians are related to earlier groups.
collocations: Azilians in the late Stone Age

senses.definition: In Jewish mysticism, the highest of four spiritual worlds, representing closeness to the divine.
examples: In Kabbalah, Azilut is the world nearest to God.
The text describes how souls descend from Azilut to lower worlds.
Scholars interpret Azilut as a symbol of pure spiritual light.

senses.definition: A historical name for the bright star Spica in the constellation Virgo.
examples: Sailors once used Azimech as a guide star.
Old manuscripts refer to Spica as Azimech.
He studied how Azimech was described in medieval astronomy.

senses.definition: A nitrogen‑containing chemical compound with a three‑membered ring, discussed mainly in specialist organic chemistry.
examples: Azimene is known for its ring strain and reactivity.
The chemist suggested that azimene might rearrange under heat.
Few natural products contain an azimene structure.

senses.definition: A reactive chemical group or compound with a three‑membered ring containing nitrogen, known mainly in advanced organic chemistry.
examples: The reaction proceeds through an azimethylene intermediate.
Azimethylene species are usually too unstable to isolate.
Theoretical studies have predicted the structure of azimethylene.

senses.definition: A chemical compound containing an azido or related nitrogen group, mentioned mainly in specialist literature.
examples: The decomposition of azimide releases nitrogen gas.
Azimide compounds must be handled with care.
They reported the first synthesis of an organic azimide.

senses.definition: A rare chemical name for a nitrogen‑containing compound related to simple amines, found mainly in older technical texts.
examples: The early literature refers to this substance as azimin.
Azimin was later renamed using modern IUPAC rules.
Only a few references to azimin exist in the database.

senses.definition: A nitrogen‑containing compound with a double bond to nitrogen, similar to an imine, used in advanced organic chemistry.
examples: Azimine intermediates are proposed in the reaction mechanism.
The chemist suggested that an azimine could be formed.
Azimines can sometimes be isolated at low temperatures.

senses.definition: Used in chemical names to show the presence of an azimine‑type nitrogen group.
examples: The azimino compound showed unusual reactivity.
They prepared several azimino derivatives for testing.
An azimino group was introduced into the molecule.

senses.definition: A benzene ring compound that carries an azimino group, known from specialist organic chemistry.
examples: Aziminobenzene served as a starting material for further reactions.
The spectrum of aziminobenzene was recorded at low temperature.
They compared aziminobenzene with related substituted benzenes.

senses.definition: The angle between a fixed direction, usually north, and the line from an observer to an object in the sky, measured along the horizon.
examples: The star has an azimuth of 120 degrees from north.
They measured the sun’s azimuth at noon.
The app shows the azimuth and altitude of the planet.

senses.definition: The horizontal direction that something points or moves in, described as an angle from a reference direction.
examples: The radar antenna slowly changed its azimuth.
Missile guidance requires precise control of azimuth and elevation.
Surveyors adjusted the azimuth of the instrument.

senses.definition: Relating to azimuth or to directions measured around the horizon.
examples: They plotted the data in an azimuthal diagram.
The telescope allows fine azimuthal adjustment.
Azimuthal variations in the signal were recorded.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to azimuth or to directions around the horizon.
examples: The antenna can rotate azimuthally.
The properties of the material are uniform azimuthally.
The field was sampled azimuthally at regular intervals.

senses.definition: Plural of azimuth: several angles describing directions around the horizon, often used for stars, satellites, or instruments.
examples: The table lists the azimuths and altitudes of visible planets.
Different azimuths were tested for the antenna.
Surveyors recorded azimuths for every station point.

senses.definition: A type of chemical compound formed by joining two molecules that each contain a carbon‑nitrogen double bond, often used as intermediates in organic synthesis.
examples: The chemist obtained an azine by condensing two aldehydes.
Azines can often be converted back to the original carbonyl compounds.
They studied the stability of different azines.

senses.definition: Plural of azine: a group of chemical compounds formed by joining two molecules that each contain a carbon‑nitrogen double bond.
examples: Several azines were prepared from the same ketone.
Azines are common intermediates in organic synthesis.
The researchers compared the colors of different azines.

senses.definition: A highly toxic organophosphate insecticide that has been widely restricted or banned in many countries.
examples: Azinphosmethyl was once used on fruit crops but raised safety concerns.
Regulations now limit exposure to azinphosmethyl.
Workers handling azinphosmethyl required special protective gear.

senses.definition: A poetic or old‑fashioned Italian‑based name for an owl, known mainly from literature.
examples: In the poem, the distant cry of the aziola is heard at night.
She wondered what kind of bird the word aziola described.
The writer used 'aziola' to give the scene a mysterious tone.

senses.definition: An Italian word meaning 'action', sometimes used in music or opera titles in English texts.
examples: The composer described his work as an 'azione teatrale'.
The programme explained that 'azione sacra' means 'sacred action'.
Scholars discussed the role of azione in early opera.

senses.definition: Plural of azione, used in English discussion of Italian musical or theatrical works.
examples: The festival presented several aziones from the Baroque period.
Early aziones often combined music, dance, and spoken text.
They compared different aziones by the same composer.

senses.definition: A small ring‑shaped organic compound containing nitrogen and oxygen, used as a reactive unit in polymer and peptide chemistry.
examples: Azlactone monomers can react easily with many nucleophiles.
The polymer contains pendant azlactone groups.
Azlactone chemistry is useful for surface modification.

senses.definition: A manufactured fibre made from regenerated proteins such as casein from milk, used in textiles.
examples: Azlon was marketed as a new kind of synthetic fibre.
The fabric contains a blend of azlon and cotton.
Some azlon fibres are made from milk proteins.

senses.definition: Plural of azlon: several types or forms of manufactured fibres made from regenerated proteins.
examples: Different azlons have been developed from plant and animal proteins.
The report compares azlons with other synthetic fibres.
Azlons were once promoted as sustainable materials.

senses.definition: Describing a compound that contains the group –N=N– linking two carbon atoms, often used in the names of dyes.
examples: Azo dyes are widely used to color textiles.
They synthesized an azo compound with bright red color.
Some azo pigments can break down into harmful substances.

senses.definition: Short for azo compound or azo dye, especially in technical contexts.
examples: The factory produces several different azoes for the paint industry.
This azo is more stable to light than earlier products.
They tested the azo for possible health risks.

senses.definition: Any bacterium of the genus Azotobacter, which can fix nitrogen from the air and lives in soil.
examples: Azobacter helps improve soil fertility by fixing nitrogen.
Farmers sometimes use preparations containing azobacter.
The scientist isolated azobacter from the rhizosphere.

senses.definition: A yellow chemical compound with two benzene rings linked by an –N=N– group, used to make dyes and as a light‑responsive switch in research.
examples: Azobenzene changes shape when exposed to light.
They attached azobenzene to the polymer to control its properties.
Azobenzene is a basic building block for many azo dyes.

senses.definition: Plural of azobenzene: a group of related compounds with two benzene rings linked by an –N=N– group.
examples: Different azobenzenes absorb light at different wavelengths.
The study examined how azobenzenes behave in solution.
Azobenzenes are common in photo‑responsive materials.

senses.definition: An organic compound related to benzil that contains an azo group, described mainly in chemical literature.
examples: Azobenzil was prepared by oxidizing the corresponding azoin.
The crystals of azobenzil were orange in color.
They studied the photochemistry of azobenzil.

senses.definition: Describing a compound that has both an azo group and a benzoic‑acid‑related structure.
examples: Azobenzoic acids can act as pH‑sensitive dyes.
They synthesized an azobenzoic derivative of the drug.
Azobenzoic compounds were tested for their optical properties.

senses.definition: An older name for azobenzene, a compound with two benzene rings linked by an –N=N– group.
examples: Early papers refer to azobenzol rather than azobenzene.
Azobenzol was first obtained in the nineteenth century.
The properties of azobenzol match those of azobenzene.

senses.definition: A synthetic black dye that contains an azo group and is used for coloring materials.
examples: The fabric was colored with azoblack.
Azoblack provides a deep, intense shade.
Some countries regulate the use of azoblack in food packaging.

senses.definition: A specialized or obsolete name for certain azo compounds, found mainly in older chemical literature.
examples: The term azoch appears only in early German texts.
Modern chemists rarely use the word azoch.
He searched for the structure behind the old name azoch.
